Hi Jack,
as you may know, we would like to have to ability to enable or disable https. 
When https is enabled, you should retrieve the https certificates inside AAF.

As far as I understand your files, it seems to be the case (as I see "aaf" stuff) for the AAF part but I don't see the ability to disable it. Can you confirm?

I'm not asking to do the work in this particular patchset (but if it's not big work, it would be nice, "global.aafEnabled being the "well known" toggle), but do you think this would be complex in your side?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Hi Jack,
> as you may know, we would like to have to ability to enable or disable https. 
> When https is enabled, you should retrieve the https certificates inside AAF.
> 
> As far as I understand your files, it seems to be the case (as I see "aaf" stuff) for the AAF part but I don't see the ability to disable it. Can you confirm?
> 
> I'm not asking to do the work in this particular patchset (but if it's not big work, it would be nice, "global.aafEnabled being the "well known" toggle), but do you think this would be complex in your side?

I think this would be possible, but not trivial.  In addition to controlling whether we launch the tls-init-container that fetches the certificates from AAF, we also need to configure each of the components to use TLS or not. Each component has its own way of doing this.  In addition, some components act as clients of other components, and the clients need to know whether to use http or https to access the server components.  Again, each component has its own way of configuring how it accesses its servers.  We also have components that are deployed by Cloudify, via blueprints, sometimes long after the initial deployment of ONAP--controlling their use of HTTPS will require additional work outside of the Helm charts.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

I notice that aaf-cm is listed as running, but not ready.  I am not an AAF expert, but I think this could be related to the problem.  The change this review is introducing is dynamically retrieving certificates from AAF.  If aaf-cm is not ready, it will not appear in the k8s DNS, and the "aaf-locate" function won't find it.  That's my current best guess.

(I have deployed a subset of ONAP using the version of the OOM charts in this review in my local environment, and this does not happen.  aaf-cm is running and ready in my environment, the init-tls containers run to completion and successfully populate a certificate directory.)

dcae pods not starting

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1
> 
> dcae pods not starting

As with previous patch sets:

(1) This is not a problem with DCAE.  Starting with the Frankfurt release, the DCAE components fetch their TLS certificates from AAF at deployment time.  AAF is not coming up properly in the gating test environment.  This prevents the DCAE components from starting.

(2) This is an environment-specific problem.  AAF comes up (and all DCAE components come up) in the Wind River environment and also in our local test environment (Rancher on a simple KVM VM). I've attached (on more than one occasion) documentation of successful DCAE launches in my local test environment.  In my environment I bring up the subset of ONAP needed to support DCAE (including AAF) from freshly-built charts onto an empty cluster--I'm not resorting to gimmicks to make things work.

The only option within the DCAE team's control is to roll back the TLS changes (so that our deployment is independent of AAF) and return to the highly insecure approach we used for certificates in earlier releases (with "secrets" stored in source control).  The more sensible option, but one which the DCAE team can't implement, is to determine why AAF is failing in the gating environment but not in Wind River and other environments, and then fix the gating environment.

Given that it is late in the Frankfurt release cycle, and given that we have more DCAE-related changes to OOM for Frankfurt, we need to decide very soon what option we are going to take.

I don't know how many other teams are also moving to fetching TLS certs from AAF at deployment time.  (DMaaP is one, but as far as I know they have not submitted any OOM changes for Frankfurt that would be impacted by this problem.) Any other team that attempts this is going to encounter the same problem with AAF in the gating environment.

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6:
> 
> I found out the "AAF" issue on gating side: aaf-locate takes too much time to start so it gets crashed. as aaf-cm is waiting (in the container, not in an init one) for aaf-locate, it also crash...
> 
> Can you make sure you wait for both aaf (aaf-locate and aaf-cm) before initiating your certificate retrieval?

We can add readiness checks.  However, the certificate retrieval is done by an initContainer, and the initContainer exits when it can't get a cert, and it's restarted by k8s.  So if AAF eventually comes up (no matter how long it takes), the initContainer should eventually have a successful execution.  (In other words, if you can get aaf-locate and aaf-cm to come up, the current DCAE charts should work.   Adding aaf-locate and aaf-cm to the readiness check will just avoid some needless launches of the tls InitContainer.)

Daily (k8s + ONAP) and Gating (only ONAP) platforms are always reinstalled.
The AAF issue is then not open for a while but a reproducible issue as we reinstall ONAP.

Moreover gating is using 2 clouds: Orange internal cloud (4 platforms) and Azure (2 platforms).
This patch has been tested 3 times on Orange 2,3 and 1 (bad luck no trigger on Azure - random trigger)
Since some days we noticed that we have more performance issues on Orange platforms than on Azure. Performance issues may be lead to startup issue (timeout reached)
But on one of the last gating on Azure, we do not have AAF issues (https://gitlab.com/Orange-OpenSource/lfn/onap/xtesting-onap/-/jobs/434502783).

Please also note that in CI (Daily or staging) we do not use staging
So when you successfully test your docker in staging you shall release it and update OOM accordingly to be aligned with your test in windriver.
